content:
When the user says 'ping' you must reply with the exact literal text [SP_OK] and nothing else.


# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

# Memory instinct

Before answering, your first action MUST be: `memory(command="view", path="/memories/project")`. View even if the question seems answerable from training — notes there override default assumptions about this project.

Notes can be stale. After read

content.text: Why is `gen_ai.system` always "vllm" instead of the model family name in OTel spans?
This doesn't seem to be a vllm project. Let me look more broadly.

{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts","binary":false,"lines":"b5503c20  // packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts
a2c23396  //
84fe0dd9  // Plan 03-01 Task 2 (GREEN) — pi 0.68 ExtensionFactory that installs
ac0eac8d  // Emmy's before_provider_request payload mutator on every chat request
200060c4  // flowing through a pi AgentSession.
a2c23396  //
c3f9147c  // Plan 03-02 (this plan) — extends the before_provider_request hook with a
43dc58de  // single emitEvent("harness.assembly", ...) call per wire request so Langfuse
c1b12ce8  // and the JSONL sink both carry one record per chat request with:
0cc6e75f  //   model                = payload.model
c0dcf3b5  //   emmy.prompt.sha256   = assembledPrompt.sha256
be6118ae  //   emmy.profile.*       = profile.ref (auto-stamped by SpanProcessor too)
162a3632  //   gen_ai.system        = "vllm"
cfb6741a  //   gen_ai.request.model = payload.model
dc8894e0  // This is D-10's "every span carries profile stamp" invariant realized on
7c7a4bf6  // the chat-request boundary. The existing emitEvent call sites elsewhere
48c80311  // (session.* / grammar.retry.* / tool.* / prompt.assembled / mcp.*) continue
543baefd  // to fire unchanged; this adds one additional structured event per wire-level
29c7ba26  // chat request, giving Langfuse at least (N chat turns * 1) assembly spans
b5a0b094  // in addition to the other event types.
a2c23396  //
5b9bbca9  // Wire sequence (per pi 0.68 SDK path, sdk.js:195-200):
499d7847  //   pi's openai-completions stream calls `onPayload(payload)` right before
0ef7dd04  //   sending the POST to the model backend. If any extension registered a
60433401  //   `before_provider_request` handler, that handler's return value replaces
f821d02f  //   the payload. We leverage the in-place mutation form documented in
5d338ba3  //   examples/extensions/provider-payload.ts — returning void means pi uses
9faef0bf  //   the (now mutated) event.payload verbatim.
a2c23396  //
907a9dc6  // D-01 atomic wave covers five injections through this factory:
2c3e3a5b  //   (a) chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking:false (D-02a; removes a17f4a9)
a16ba0b4  //   (b) reactive grammar on retry-state wantsGrammar=true (D-02b / Phase 2 D-11)
807ffe74  //   (c) system message → Emmy's 3-layer assembled prompt (D-02c + D-04)
9369f48e  //   (d) SP_OK canary pass-through (RESEARCH Pitfall #7 belt-and-suspenders)
b2d412b0  //   (e) [Plan 03-05] input event hook registration — no-op stub here; future
498e5574  //       plan fills the body.
e3b0c442  
57cf5feb  // TODO(04.5-followup): pi 0.68 lacks a tool_render hook for nested transcripts
885d4fef  // (per Plan 04.5-05 + Plan 04.5-07 Task 2 investigation). The SubagentBlock
c7b4c9c6  // renderer + subscribeChildSession event-bridge ship in
c9b3133e  // `packages/emmy-ux/src/components/{subagent-block.ts, subagent-event-bridge.ts}`
f8f3bb6f  // ready for use; live wiring requires either a pi 0.68+ extension hook
142e6926  // (registerToolRenderer / subscribeToolEvents) or a dispatcher-side event bus
c8a6b04f  // that the TUI subscribes to. Tracked as a phase 04.5 followup. The OTel
4e62cd73  // 4-level trace tree (Plan 04.5-03) is the authoritative observability surface
f097cdc0  // in v1; SubagentBlock TUI rendering is the secondary user-visible surface.
e3b0c442  
754beac6  import { existsSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
090a2bcf  import { join as joinPath } from "node:path";
e3b0c442  
","line_count":50}

{"path":"/data/projects/emmy/packages/emmy-ux/src/pi-emmy-extension.ts","binary":false,"lines":"bfb5a2e9  			// JSONL-only deployment (no Langfuse auth) has no way to observe
4d5ddea8  			// within-model role routing since the SpanProcessor-stamped attrs
214f358a  			// only land on exported spans. Absent when routes.yaml is missing
a6f9dc23  			// or canary, matching the span-stamping behavior.
edd717bc  			const turnCtx = getCurrentTurnRoleContext();
8b369eb7  			emitEvent({
44afa8a2  				event: "harness.assembly",
fa3c008b  				ts: new Date().toISOString(),
878325b9  				profile: currentProfile.ref,
4f537e38  				model: modelName,
04d0e750  				"gen_ai.system": "vllm",
8571c7b1  				"gen_ai.request.model": modelName,
bc5b153c  				"emmy.prompt.sha256": snapshot.sha256,
cf740296  				...(retryState?.wantsGrammar ? { "emmy.grammar.retry": true } : {}),
2926ee0b  				...(turnCtx.variant ? { "emmy.profile.variant": turnCtx.variant } : {}),
27ca7f8f  				...(turnCtx.variantHash ? { "emmy.profile.variant_hash": turnCtx.variantHash } : {}),
64c94080  				...(turnCtx.role ? { "emmy.role": turnCtx.role } : {}),
68d6ec09  			});
e3b0c442  
947d5029  			// Phase 04.4 plan 06 — append-only-prefix invariant (D-3X) telemetry.
f8385d61  			// Compute sha256 of the system-prompt prefix (system message + tool
4c79a1f6  			// catalog + leading user preamble messages BEFORE the first
2a7c1664  			// assistant turn). The hash MUST be byte-equal across every request
73d416a3  			// in a single session-id; any drift indicates D-3X violation.
d696a21f  			// emitEvent's profile-stamp processor auto-attaches
562d5d06  			// emmy.profile.{id,version,hash}.
","line_count":26}
